items.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask

children.content: Concerns about anti-social behaviour, construction and poor lighting are shaping perceptions of safety in Civic, but locals remain divided about whether Canberra's CBD feels unsafe after dark.

gallery.altText: An unknown person walks past a graffiti tunnel.

thumbnail.altText: An unknown person walks past a graffiti tunnel.

items.title: Bingo player awarded $285k after tripping on cracked tile at Canberra club

children.content: The woman, who was awarded $285,000, rated her pain as 10 out of 10 and spent three nights in hospital.

image.altText: The exterior of a club.

items.title: Economist blames ACT's fiscal deterioration entirely on 'policy decisions'

children.content: An independent economist warns Canberrans will need to pay higher taxes and charges if the ACT government continues the same amount of spending on infrastructure and services.

image.altText: A man with glasses and a suit and tie sits in a light green chair surrounded by books in his personal library.

items.title: 'Numbers don't add up': Union slams ACT plan to boost paramedics, firefighters

children.content: The ACT government has unveiled a $94.3 million emergency services package, but the union says it still falls short by about 50 staff needed to prevent future station closures.

items.title: 'Better for victims': $18.9 million invested in director of public prosecutions

children.content: The funding will go towards 24 new, permanent positions within the office to meet its increasing case load, Attorney-General Tara Cheyne said.

image.altText: Canberra's coat of arms

items.title: Woman dies as fire in north Canberra unit causes 'substantial damage'

children.content: Emergency services were called to the Wallcliffe Flats in Belconnen at about 6:30am where they found a ground-floor unit alight.

image.altText: A fire truck and personnel.

items.title: Former Cuban president Raúl Castro indicted on murder charges in US

children.content: The move is the latest escalation in Washington's pressure campaign against the island's communist government.

gallery.altText: Raul Castro in tan brown military clothing, dark sunglasses and a cap, smiling with a hand raised over his eyes.

thumbnail.altText: Raul Castro in tan brown military clothing, dark sunglasses and a cap, smiling with a hand raised over his eyes.

items.title: Aged care refunds for overcharging a 'burden' and 'total joke,' advocates say

children.content: Some advocates say the government's decision to delay price caps for at-home aged care services is a "burden".

image.altText: A man wearing a collared shirt and cardigan sits in a house. He looks into camera with a serious expression.

items.title: Trump's pick for ambassador to Australia names three priorities for role

children.content: David Brat, who rose to political prominence as part of the conservative Tea Party movement, takes questions from senators at a confirmation hearing.

image.altText: David Brat sits at a desk and speaks.

items.title: Israeli minister taunts detained activists from Global Sumud Flotilla in video

children.content: Some of the detained activists were being pushed and dragged along the ground by Israeli forces while Itamar Ben-Gvir was taunting them.

gallery.altText: people kneeling on the ground as armed soldiers watch over

items.title: Photographer accused of stealing Bondi terror attack victim's camera

children.content: A photographer who allegedly stole a camera from a Bondi terror attack victim before pawning it days later has been charged.

image.altText: A man who's face is blurred is escorted by a police officer.

items.title: 'Survival mode all the time': Australian parents break taboos on raising kids

children.content: Almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national survey of parents as the birth rate hits a record low

image.altText: A middle aged woman with long brown hair sitting at a desk with a laptop

items.title: They might look the same, but these penguins are very different

children.content: Gentoo penguins were thought to be a standalone species, but a new study says there are four distinct species — and it could lead to better conservation plans.

gallery.altText: Three small penguins with bright red-orange bills and conspicuous white eye patches.
A woman in a purple beanie standing in front of a colony of king penguins.
A penguin colony in an Antarctic landscape.

A penguin colony in an Antarctic landscape.

items.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?

children.content: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.

image.altText: Green crops in the foreground with machinery in the background.

items.title: Inside the 'worst managed infrastructure project in Australian history'

children.content: The Inland Rail has had funding slashed and the project placed on pause but Everald Compton has a bottle of Penfolds Grange waiting and even if it takes him till the age of 100, he's determined to see it through.

image.altText: Old man looking at model train.

items.title: How to improve your home without stepping inside

children.content: Adding a new kitchen or bathroom to your home can be expensive, but budget constraints don't mean you have to take home improvement off the table altogether.

gallery.altText: two lush garden beds outside a small cottage.
A back yard with a firepit and a deck leading to a plunge pool.

items.title: Can The Mandalorian and Grogu save Star Wars?

children.content: Grogu (aka Baby Yoda) made The Mandalorian a hit on streaming, now Disney is looking to parlay that goodwill into ticket sales to the latest Star Wars cinematic release.

image.altText: Four small creatures ride in a cart in the desert

items.title: Think you know music and film? Let's see how well you score in this quiz

children.content: From Mozart to Max Richter, can you pick the classical music in these iconic films?

image.altText: A man in a yellow space suit walks through a white hexagonal corridor on a space station.

items.title: A year on, survivors still counting cost of '1-in-500-year' disaster

children.content: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mis North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.

gallery.altText: Man with white hair and beard sits on a sunny back veranda with a cup in hand and garden behind him.
Furniture, mattress, books and documents piled up in a heap ruined by floodwater and mud.
A country road covered in a thick layer of mud after a flood in a country town.

items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed. Here's why

children.content: The youngest of the Snowtown killers was the subject of a long-running suppression order that kept his face — or any description of his appearance — hidden for 25 years. Here's why that's been lifted.

image.altText: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis in custody.

items.title: WA gas users warn Labor not to repeat flaws in state reservation scheme

children.content: Western Australian gas users are urging the Albanese government to build stronger transparency and enforcement into its proposed national gas reserve scheme.

image.altText: Aerial shot of massive oil and gas rig in the Bass Strait off Victoria

items.title: Recycling contractor accused of having employees sleep on floor sues for $5m

children.content: Allegations that a depot operator in Queensland's container exchange recycling program conducted itself in a manner that could be likened to modern slavery have been detailed during a court hearing in a multi-million dollar federal court dispute.

image.altText: A birds eye view of green sheds.

items.title: Pendlebury's 433rd game simply his latest of many AFL records

children.content: Scott Pendlebury is set to be the proud holder of the AFL's games record, but it's only the latest in a long line of records held by the Collingwood icon.

image.altText: Scott Pendlebury clenches his fist in celebration of a goal

items.title: Victorian town braces for impact of impending Bega Cheese factory closure

children.content: Bega Cheese's Strathmerton factory, which employed more than 300 workers, is set to close its doors on June 30, leaving residents in the small Victorian town wondering what's next.

items.title: Man charged with kidnapping Marvin Oraiha months before his alleged murder

items.title: Putin's China visit looked like Trump's but couldn't have been more different

children.content: Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping agreed to a raft of deals in front of invited press less than a week after Donald Trump left China's capital with no confirmed deals.

items.title: Greenland tells US again it is 'not for sale' after Trump envoy talks

items.title: Child traumatised by family violence now learning 'new words every day'

children.content: When Claire arrived at a women's refuge in outer Sydney with her four-year-old daughter in tow, the child was "barely able to speak clearly".

image.altText: A detail of a mother's hand holding her little daughter's hand with grey and pink sleeves.

items.title: 'Short-term pain' to wrestle Tasmanian budget back to black in two years

children.content: There will be "short-term pain" to get Tasmania's finances back in the black within two years, state treasurer Eric Abetz says ahead of today's budget.

image.altText: Politicians sitting in a parliament room, listening to one older guy speak.

items.title: Trawlers may sit idle but residents are holding onto Karumba's future

children.content: Karumba's rich fishing industry dates back to the roaring, prawning era of the 60s, but major blows are seeing a seafood decline. However, the Gulf of Carpentaria residents say they "wouldn't live anywhere else".

items.title: Alcaraz injury woes deepen, former champion out of Wimbledon

children.content: Carlos Alcaraz will miss Wimbledon as he continues his rehab from a wrist injury that has already ruled him out of the French Open.
